data.title: Ciena outlines 14% revenue growth target for fiscal 2025 as cloud orders accelerate
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Ciena Corporation (CIEN) Q2 2025

Management View

  • Gary B. Smith, President and CEO, highlighted strong second quarter results with revenue of $1.13 billion, reaching the high end of guidance, and emphasized that "revenue from cloud providers stood out as a key driver in Q2." He noted a record direct cloud provider revenue comprising 38% of total revenue, growing 85% year-over-year and surpassing $400 million in a single quarter for the first time. Smith stated that "orders in the quarter were again significantly greater than revenue," and Ciena is "on track for cloud provider orders to double in fiscal 2025 over last year." He also detailed two significant new data center-related wins: a strategic win for connecting regional GPU clusters and another for out-of-band network management for a global cloud provider, both expected to generate incremental revenue starting later in fiscal 2025 and ramping into 2026.

  • Smith reported momentum across service providers, noting "several new customer wins in both the Americas and international regions, including Europe," and all-time record MOFN activity in the first half of fiscal 2025.

  • The software business saw notable gains as "orders for Navigator increased significantly in the first half of fiscal 2025 by more than 30% year-over-year," and Blue Planet achieved its highest ever quarterly revenue at just under $30 million.

  • James E. Moylan, CFO, stated, "Total revenue in Q2 was $1.13 billion. This included 2 10%-plus customers, 1 cloud provider and 1 service provider." Moylan highlighted adjusted gross margin of 41%, adjusted operating expense of $369 million, adjusted operating margin of 8.2%, adjusted net income of $61 million, and adjusted EPS of $0.42. He also noted, "We generated $157 million in cash from operations" and ended the quarter with approximately $1.35 billion in cash and investments.

  • Moylan shared that Ciena added 24 new WaveLogic 6 Extreme customers in Q2, bringing the total to 49 within two quarters of general availability, and remains on target to double year-over-year revenue from coherent pluggable optics to at least $150 million in fiscal 2025.

  • The company acknowledged Moylan's upcoming retirement, marking his last earnings call as CFO.

Outlook

  • Ciena expects Q3 revenue in the range of $1.13 billion to $1.21 billion, with adjusted gross margin projected to be "roughly in line with Q2" and adjusted operating expense of approximately $370 million to $375 million. For full fiscal 2025, management now targets revenue growth of approximately 14%. Moylan explained Ciena expects annual gross margins at the lower end of the previously assumed 42% to 44% range, citing a higher proportion of newly introduced solutions and RLS ramping.

  • Incentive compensation is expected to increase by approximately $10 million per quarter due to strong financial performance, with operating expense averaging $360 million to $370 million per quarter for the year.

Financial Results

  • Ciena reported $1.13 billion in revenue, adjusted gross margin of 41%, adjusted operating expense of $369 million, adjusted operating margin of 8.2%, adjusted net income of $61 million, and adjusted EPS of $0.42. Cash from operations reached $157 million, and adjusted EBITDA was $117 million. The company repurchased approximately 1.2 million shares for $84 million during the quarter.

  • Optical segment performance was strong, with 24 additional WaveLogic 6 Extreme customers and continued adoption of WaveLogic 5 solutions. The routing and switching business secured a significant win with a Tier 1 service provider in India and introduced the first 800-gig router to its portfolio.

Q&A

  • Samik Chatterjee, JPMorgan, asked about order momentum and sustainability. Smith responded that "we saw strong order flows in Q1. That continued in Q2 and probably accelerated in Q2 and the activity," describing demand from both service providers and cloud customers as durable and noting incremental opportunities from GPU clusters.

  • Chatterjee also questioned margin headwinds from pluggables and new products. Moylan answered, "We've always said that mix is the single most important element of our gross margin number in any given quarter," adding that pluggables and RLS are currently below corporate average margins but expected to improve with cost reductions and scale.

  • Simon Leopold, Raymond James, inquired about top customer contributions and long-term cloud trends. Moylan specified, "The largest customer was a cloud customer, it was 13%... the second one was our service provider, AT&T at 10.4%. The top 5 customers were 45% of revenue." Smith described a broadening set of cloud providers and deepening relationships, giving "confidence in the sustainability and durability" of growth.

  • Amit Daryanani, Evercore, asked about the timing of order conversion and pluggables opportunity. Smith indicated that much of the order momentum will convert to revenue in fiscal 2026, with a larger backlog expected entering the year. He characterized the GPU cluster opportunity as "hundreds of millions of dollars" and "very sizable."

  • Other analysts focused on Blue Planet's AI-related momentum, routing/switching wins, margin trajectory, MOFN pipeline expansion, and tariff mitigation strategies. Management highlighted strong order books, broad adoption of new technologies, and ongoing efforts to offset tariff impacts.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed optimism about order momentum, cloud wins, and backlog growth, while seeking clarification on gross margin pressures and revenue conversion timing. The tone was slightly positive, with most questions probing for long-term sustainability and incremental growth.

  • Management maintained a confident and upbeat tone during prepared remarks, emphasizing "strong momentum," "validation from customers," and "confidence that we can strategically expand our market opportunity." In Q&A, executives remained measured but reassuring, especially when addressing margin headwinds and order linearity.

  • Compared to the previous quarter, both analysts and management displayed increased confidence. Management's tone shifted from cautious optimism to strong conviction about growth prospects, while analysts focused more on durability and margin trajectory.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for fiscal 2025 revenue growth was raised to approximately 14%, up from the prior 8% to 11% range, reflecting stronger-than-expected order flow and backlog. Gross margin outlook narrowed to the lower end of 42% to 44%, citing product mix impacts, whereas the previous quarter expected a slightly higher margin range. Operating expense guidance increased, driven by higher incentive compensation.

  • Strategic focus intensified on cloud provider market share, GPU cluster connectivity, and pluggables. Analysts in the current quarter probed more deeply into order sustainability, margin headwinds, and timing of revenue conversion, shifting from previous questions about inventory digestion and recovery pacing. Management's confidence in AI-driven demand and new applications increased compared to last quarter.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management acknowledged a dynamic tariff environment, but Moylan noted, "we are assuming that the current tariff structure does not change" and expects "the net effect to our bottom line in future quarters will be immaterial."

  • Gross margin pressure from a high mix of pluggables and RLS was cited as a challenge, with improvement expected as cost points decline and product mix shifts. Order conversion timing and backlog growth were also highlighted as factors impacting near-term financials.

  • Analysts raised concerns about sustainability of cloud demand, margin headwinds from product mix, and timing of backlog conversion to revenue.

Final Takeaway

Ciena's second quarter results underscore accelerating demand from cloud providers, highlighted by record direct revenue and a strengthened backlog that positions the company for sustained growth. Management's updated outlook targets 14% revenue growth for fiscal 2025, driven by broad adoption of its portfolio in cloud and service provider segments, new wins in data center-related applications, and robust order momentum. Short-term gross margin headwinds from product mix and tariffs are being actively managed, with improvement expected as new products scale and cost efficiencies are realized. The company enters the second half of the year with increased confidence in its long-term growth trajectory and ability to capitalize on AI-driven infrastructure investments.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Bernstein begins coverage of the U.S. entertainment sector
data.description:
Analysts at Bernstein started coverage of the U.S. entertainment sector and have picked out seven stocks that they believe stand to benefit from "superfans," who will drive growth & profitability.

"We believe that the ability to optimally attract and monetize superfans will be the key driver and differentiator of both growth and profitability in this industry. Superfans include high-earning or wealthy individuals, are less sensitive to price increases, and exhibit resilient spending habits during recessions... We believe superfan-driven growth will be sustained for years to come," the analysts said.

Live Nation (NYSE:LYV) (Outperform, $185 PT, 35% upside potential): The research firm is bullish on LYV due to its powerful flywheel, which has the potential to drive meaningful outperformance across each of its operating segments. They believe that recent public and political pressure has come from a misunderstanding of the business and see the potential for multiple expansions as investor concerns ease. 

"Technological improvements coupled with changes in artist and consumer behavior, especially among younger generations, enhance the inherent scarcity value of live experiences and position Ticketmaster to grow GTV ahead of expectations into the summer concert season and beyond," the research firm said.

Spotify (NYSE:SPOT) (Outperform, $825 PT, 24% upside potential): Bernstein analysts see a strong investment case for the music streaming giant due to its proven ability to leverage its sizable market share and quality platform. 

"We believe the business can exercise pricing power sustainably given recent success with minimal churn and expect regular price hikes to become a consistent ARPU driver. We expect the stock to outperform on further gross margin expansion and the launch of innovative and unique features, including a superfan subscription tie," the research firm said.

DraftKings (NASDAQ:DKNG) (Outperform, $46 PT, 28% upside potential): Bernstein analysts noted that DraftKings' enhanced live pricing capabilities, which are significantly boosted by the Simplebet acquisition, are the core lever toward capturing substantial growth in U.S. live betting and driving strong handle and profitability growth. 

"DKNG has also demonstrated a unique ability to capitalize on event-driven engagement, which we believe should drive accelerating live bettor acquisition as streamers begin to distribute an increasing volume of live events," the research firm said.

TKO Group (NYSE:TKO) (Outperform, $190 PT, 20% upside potential): The research firm believes TKO represents an attractive investment in live sports entertainment with a clear strategy for sustained growth. In the near term, they expect the UFC rights renewal to exceed $1B annually, and transformation at WWE to continue to drive efficiency gains and cost savings.

"We believe the core owned and operated media properties offer compelling long-term growth prospects around media rights, live events, and sponsorship, while IMG and OLE benefit from the same secular themes and provide exposure to opportunities within other rapidly growing corners of the sports ecosystem," the analysts said.

Warner Music Group (NASDAQ:WMG) (Outperform, $32 PT, 22% upside potential): They think the company offers an attractive investment opportunity as its strong new management team executes a clear strategy toward monetizing its unique slate of IP. 

"The new management team is meeting strong secular tailwinds in recording and publishing that are just beginning to deliver. Looking ahead to 2026, we see a compelling reacceleration in digital revenue, market share gains, and margin expansion," Bernstein analysts said.

Liberty Formula One (NASDAQ:FWONA) (NASDAQ:FWONK) (Market-Perform, $105 PT, 9% upside potential): "While we do see significant opportunity for F1 to expand U.S. viewership and attendance, we expect the potential catalysts for an inflection to come in 2027 and beyond. In the meantime, we believe F1 media rights and sponsorship revenues have an attractive growth runway, which, along with the integration of MotoGP after a mid-year Dorna close, underwrites the current valuation," the research firm said.

Flutter Entertainment (NYSE:FLUT) (Market-Perform, $275 PT, 9% upside potential): "The company laid out a compelling growth story with attractive revenue, EBITDA, and cash flow targets for 2027 at the investor day. While we believe these targets are achievable, we also believe that earnings expectations are now roughly in line. We see underappreciated upside to expectations for Brazil & Italy, but we are incrementally less optimistic around SGP penetration and UK gaming levies," the research firm said.

data.title: Liminatus Pharma stock gains on regaining compliance with Nasdaq
data.description:
• Liminatus Pharma (NASDAQ:LIMN) shares rose after the company said it regained compliance with Nasdaq's listing needs.
  • The stock is up 43.5%.
  • The company, last month, had received a notice of non-compliance from the stock exchange.
  • On 2nd June, Liminatus filed its 10-Q, confirming its doubt in the ability to continue as a going concern.

data.title: Lynas shares surge to two-year high on risks from China's rare earth export restrictions
data.description:
Lynas (OTCPK:LYSCF) (OTCPK:LYSDY) +10% in Thursday's trading to their highest level since February 2023 after global automakers warned that China's export restrictions on rare earth materials could lead to production delays, according to Reuters.

Analysts said Lynas (OTCPK:LYSCF) (OTCPK:LYSDY), as the world's largest rare earth producing company outside China, could benefit from the conditions in an environment of rising geopolitical tension and demand for critical minerals.

"As China tightens rare-earth export controls, markets are pricing in supply risks, positioning Lynas... as a strategic hedge," Vantage Markets analyst Hebe Chen told Reuters.

China, which accounts for 90% of global rare earth production, imposed export restrictions in April on the strategic minerals in response to President Trump's tariffs, raising alarms across industries reliant on the elements that are critical for defense systems, electric vehicles, clean energy, and advanced electronics.

This week, German automakers added urgency to the concerns, warning that China's export restrictions on rare earths pose a significant threat to their production lines and local economies.

data.title: Market Voices: Musk attacks GOP spending bill; ECB cuts interest rates
data.description:
Seeking Alpha's daily roundup of statements and remarks by newsmakers that might impact markets, sectors or individual stocks.

-Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) CEO and Trump supporter Elon Musk continued to criticize the GOP-backed budget bill currently making its way through Congress.

"A new spending bill should be drafted that doesn’t massively grow the deficit and increase the debt ceiling by 5 TRILLION DOLLARS," Musk posted on X on Wednesday afternoon. 

Musk later posted, "Call your Senator, Call your Congressman, Bankrupting America is NOT ok! KILL the BILL."

-The European Central Bank said it was lowering three key interest rates by 25 basis points, bringing the deposit facility rate down to 2%. It added that inflation was now at around its 2% medium-term target. 

"The decision to lower the deposit facility rate – the rate through which the Governing Council steers the monetary policy stance – is based on its updated assessment of the inflation outlook, the dynamics of underlying inflation and the strength of monetary policy transmission,” the ECB said in a statement on Thursday. 

-Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said at a NATO meeting on Thursday that the U.S. is expecting member states to raise their individual contributions to 5% of their respective GDPs. Currently, members are only required to contribute 2%, with several falling short of that goal in recent years.

“We’re here to continue the work that President Trump started, which is a commitment to 5% defense spending across this alliance, which, we think, will happen,” Hegseth told reporters, according to CNBC.

data.title: Arlo Technologies stock soars on, surpassing $300M in ARR
data.description:
• Arlo Technologies (NYSE:ARLO) has now joined the ranks of elite public companies by surpassing $300M in annual recurring revenue, driven by continued growth from subscription services delivered by Arlo's AI-powered smart security platform.
  • Underpinning this growth is Arlo's globally-scaled SaaS platform with Arlo Intelligence™, Arlo's groundbreaking AI engine that enables customers to activate, tailor and manage their smart security ecosystem that adapts and grows as needs change.
  • "Today's announcement is the result of the tireless pursuit and exceptional focus of everyone at Arlo committed to making smart security accessible for anyone, at any stage of life." said Matthew McRae, CEO.
  • Arlo continues to outpace its peers through its relentless commitment to innovation and operational excellence.
  • In commemoration of Arlo's milestone achievement, Arlo has been invited to host the opening bell at the NYSE on July 9, 2025.
  • ARLO is +6.99% to $15.61.

data.title: Trump says phone call with China's Xi 'resulted in a very positive conclusion'
data.description:
U.S. President Donald Trump on Thursday said that he had held a "good phone call" with China's leader Xi Jinping which "resulted in a very positive conclusion" for the world's two largest economies. Trump said there "should no longer be any questions" over rare earths, and that he had been invited by Xi to visit China. 

"The conversation was focused almost entirely on TRADE. Nothing was discussed concerning Russia/Ukraine, or Iran. We will inform the Media as to scheduling and location of the soon to be meeting," the President said on his Truth Social service. 

In the morning, Xinhua and CNN had reported that the two Presidents were holding a phone call. Xinhua added that the call had been requested by Trump. 

The White House responded to an earlier request for comment from Seeking Alpha with Trump's Truth Social post.      

U.S. stock index futures rose sharply on Xinhua's report, but quickly gave up those gains. Stocks have fluctuated after the opening bell. 

Here are some exchange-traded funds that track the benchmark S&P 500 index (SP500): (NYSEARCA:SPY), (NYSEARCA:VOO), (NYSEARCA:IVV), (NYSEARCA:RSP), (NYSEARCA:SSO), (NYSEARCA:UPRO), (NYSEARCA:SH), (NYSEARCA:SDS), and (NYSEARCA:SPXU).

And here are some China-linked ETFs: (NYSEARCA:KWEB), (NASDAQ:PGJ), (NYSEARCA:CQQQ), (NYSEARCA:FXI), (NYSEARCA:GXC), (NASDAQ:MCHI), (NYSEARCA:FLCH), (BATS:CNYA), (NYSEARCA:ASHR), and (NYSEARCA:YINN).

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Circle shares indicated to open as much as 42% above IPO price - report
data.description:
Circle Internet Group (NYSE:CRCL) stock is indicated to debut at $42-$44 per share on Thursday, representing a 36%-42% premium relative to the $31 initial public offering price, Bloomberg reported.

The stablecoin issuer and some of its backers raised $1.1B in the offering, selling 34M shares for $31 each. That was upsized from the previous marketed range of $27-$28 after being boosted once before amid strong demand.

Shares are slated to start trading Thursday on the New York Stock Exchange.

Circle (NYSE:CRCL) is the issuer of USD Coin (USDC-USD), the second-largest stablecoin by market cap.

A stablecoin is a type of crypto designed to maintain a stable value by being pegged to a real-world asset like the U.S. dollar or gold.

data.title: Harmony Biosciences, Lupin reach agreement on Wakix generic launch
data.description:
• Lupin can launch a generic version of Harmony Biosciences' (NASDAQ:HRMY) Wakix (pitolisant) in January 2030 under a settlement agreement.
  • The agreement resolves patent infringement litigation filed by Harmony against Lupin regarding its ANDA for generic Wakix, a treatment for excessive daytime sleepiness.
  • Harmony noted that it is currently working on new formulations of pitolisant with utility patents that could provide exclusivity through 2044.
